summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an Oct‑22‑89 show with a soaring Birdsong, stellar post‑Franklin’s space jam and solid second‑set jams, though Blow Away falls flat.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑mastered soundboard tape that lets the band’s late‑‘80s resurgence shine. The second set delivers highlights – a 15‑minute Birdsong, an inspired post‑Franklin’s space jam, Slipknot and Help>Slip>Franklin’s sequences, plus strong performances from Jerry on his “Wolf” guitar. The first set is uneven and Blow Away is consistently called out as weak, but the overall show is regarded as a solid example of the 1989 fall tour.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical peaks and the pristine soundboard source; only a few note specific song flaws (Blow Away) and some broader era‑bias criticism, yielding a strong but not flawless overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as ultra‑clean, sterile, top‑quality mastering (Charlie Miller). No major dropouts; occasional comments about “rough spots” refer to performance rather than audio.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set considered uneven/so‑so, but the second set is a showcase of extended jams and tight transitions; unusual placement of Help>Slip>Franklin’s works well.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Comfortable on his “Wolf” guitar all tour; solos highlighted on Queen Jane and praised for melodic “gossamer buttress notes”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Some reviewers felt Lesh’s presence was diminished compared to earlier years.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work criticized on Blow Away (forgot lyrics) but noted for using MIDI during the tour.
band_member_comments.Drums: Space jam after Franklin's praised; drums contribute strongly to the post‑Franklin’s improvisation.
